simple-reasoning

10.0/10
📝 Prompt: 
If a train travels 60 miles per hour for 2 hours, how far does it go?
📋 Criteria: 
Answer is 120 miles with clear calculation shown
💬 Model Response: 
To find out how far the train goes, you can use the formula:

\[ \text{Distance} = \text{Speed} \times \text{Time} \]

Given that the speed is 60 miles per hour and the time is 2 hours, you would calculate it as follows:

\[ \text{Distance} = 60 \, \text{miles/hour} \times 2 \, \text{hours} = 120 \, \text{miles} \]

So, the train travels 120 miles.
⚖️ Judge Reasoning:
The response correctly calculates the distance and addresses all criteria succinctly.
Accuracy
10/10
Completeness
10/10
Conciseness
10/10
